content.claim: Hungary's centralized governance (Klebelsberg Centre/Tankerület system) and centralized entrance-exam infrastructure (Oktatási Hivatal) would remain structurally unchanged by any reform of 6/8-year gimnázium tracks
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Establishes what governance structures are independent of track decision, not their causal role in segregation
Does not address whether centralized exam criteria themselves drive or mitigate inequitable selection
Assumes no reform to entrance-exam accessibility or criteria would accompany track restructuring
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
